Use Speed Up to resend with higher fees: Use Speed Up to resend with higher fees or Cancel to submit a 0-value self-transfer using the same nonce.
%@ addresses are hex and typically start: %@ addresses are hex and typically start with 0x.
%@ addresses use EVM format (0x + 40 hex: %@ addresses use EVM format (0x + 40 hex characters).
%@ fiat exchange rates are unavailable. Using the last successful rates.

%@ history refresh failed. Using cached: %@ history refresh failed. Using cached history.

%@ providers are partially reachable. So: %@ providers are partially reachable. Some balances are estimated from confirmed on-chain history.
%@ providers are unavailable. Using bala: %@ providers are unavailable. Using balance estimated from confirmed on-chain history.
%@ providers are unavailable. Using cach: %@ providers are unavailable. Using cached balances and history.
%@ refresh timed out. Using cached balan: %@ refresh timed out. Using cached balances and history.
Add a custom token contract, mint addres: Add a custom token contract, mint address, coin type, package address, account ID, or jetton master address for Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, BNB Chain, Avalanche, Hyperliquid, Solana, Sui, Aptos, TON, NEAR, or Tron.
Adjust how many decimals are shown for e: Adjust how many decimals are shown for each chain's native asset. Very small values switch to a threshold marker instead of rounding to zero.
Alert added. Spectra will notify you whe: Alert added. Spectra will notify you when this target is hit.
Amount is below Dogecoin dust threshold.
An Ethereum RPC endpoint is required for: An Ethereum RPC endpoint is required for live chain access.
An identical alert already exists for th: An identical alert already exists for this asset.
Bitcoin Cash broadcast returned an empty: Bitcoin Cash broadcast returned an empty transaction hash.
Bitcoin SV broadcast returned an empty t: Bitcoin SV broadcast returned an empty transaction hash.
Browse Spectra's supported chains, defau: Browse Spectra's supported chains, default derivation paths, registered SLIP44 coin types, and protocol-level notes in a cleaner reference format.
Calculated change is below dust threshol: Calculated change is below dust threshold. Increase amount or consolidate UTXOs.
Cancellation context loaded. Review fees: Cancellation context loaded. Review fees and tap Send.
Cardano addresses typically start with a: Cardano addresses typically start with addr1 and use bech32 format.
Choose a longer interval if you want low: Choose a longer interval if you want lower background activity and less battery impact.
Choose a wallet and asset to prepare a t: Choose a wallet and asset to prepare a transfer with live fee previews and risk checks.
Choose custom artwork, your own photo, o: Choose custom artwork, your own photo, or the classic generated badge style. Uploaded images must be 3 MB or smaller.
Choose how often Spectra refreshes balan: Choose how often Spectra refreshes balances automatically while the app is active.
Choose which categories to remove from t: Choose which categories to remove from this device. Selected items are deleted locally and some options also clear secure keychain data.
Choose which providers Spectra is allowe: Choose which providers Spectra is allowed to broadcast through for this chain. At least one provider must remain enabled.
Could not verify %@ contract bytecode on: Could not verify %@ contract bytecode on %@.
Could not verify recipient contract stat: Could not verify recipient contract state on %@. Review destination carefully.
Create alert rules for imported assets. When the current price reaches your target, Spectra sends a local notification. Alerts depend on price refreshes from your selected pricing source and fall back to built-in prices when live data is unavailable. Spectra refreshes prices when the app becomes active and on a repeating in-app watch cycle while it stays open.
Current wallet, pricing, and chain-state: Current wallet, pricing, and chain-state warnings will appear here.
Custom EIP-1559 fees are applied to this: Custom EIP-1559 fees are applied to this send and preview.
DOGE transaction marked failed after ext: DOGE transaction marked failed after extended retries.
Destination appears to be a non-EVM addr: Destination appears to be a non-EVM address while sending on %@.
Destination appears to be an Ethereum-st: Destination appears to be an Ethereum-style address while sending on %@.
Destination appears to be non-Monero for: Destination appears to be non-Monero format while sending on Monero.
Destination appears to be non-Solana for: Destination appears to be non-Solana format while sending on Solana.
Destination appears to be non-Tron forma: Destination appears to be non-Tron format while sending on Tron.
Destination appears to be non-XRP format: Destination appears to be non-XRP format while sending on XRP Ledger.
Dogecoin addresses usually start with D,: Dogecoin addresses usually start with D, A, or 9.
Dogecoin transaction appears stuck and c: Dogecoin transaction appears stuck and could not be confirmed after extended retries.
Dogecoin transaction is too large for st: Dogecoin transaction is too large for standard relay policy.
ENS name '%@' resolved to %@. Confirm th: ENS name '%@' resolved to %@. Confirm this resolved address before sending.
ENS names are Ethereum-specific. For %@,: ENS names are Ethereum-specific. For %@, verify the resolved EVM address very carefully.
ERC-20 and TRC-20 tokens expose decimals: ERC-20 and TRC-20 tokens expose decimals on the contract, and Solana tokens store decimals on the mint account. Manage tracked token decimal support separately from native asset display precision.

For Bitcoin Cash sends, advanced mode re: For Bitcoin Cash sends, advanced mode records RBF intent and applies the max-input cap for coin selection.
For Bitcoin sends, advanced mode records: For Bitcoin sends, advanced mode records RBF/CPFP intent and applies the max-input cap for coin selection.
For Dogecoin sends, advanced mode record: For Dogecoin sends, advanced mode records RBF/CPFP intent and applies the max-input cap for coin selection.
For LTC sends, max input cap is applied: For LTC sends, max input cap is applied for coin selection, RBF policy is encoded in input sequence numbers, and change strategy controls whether change uses a derived change path or your source address.

Insufficient TON balance to cover amount: Insufficient TON balance to cover amount and network fee.

Solana addresses are Base58 encoded and: Solana addresses are Base58 encoded and typically 32-44 characters.
Spectra can notify you when your total p: Spectra can notify you when your total portfolio moves beyond your configured thresholds.
Spectra is resolving the current address: Spectra is resolving the current address for this wallet.
Spectra signs and broadcasts ADA transfe: Spectra signs and broadcasts ADA transfers in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts Dogecoin in: Spectra signs and broadcasts Dogecoin in-app. The preview shows estimated network fee and max sendable DOGE for this wallet.
Spectra signs and broadcasts ICP transfe: Spectra signs and broadcasts ICP transfers in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts NEAR transf: Spectra signs and broadcasts NEAR transfers in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts Polkadot tr: Spectra signs and broadcasts Polkadot transfers in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts Stellar pay: Spectra signs and broadcasts Stellar payments in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts Tron transf: Spectra signs and broadcasts Tron transfers in-app, including TRX and TRC-20 USDT.
Spectra signs and broadcasts XRP transfe: Spectra signs and broadcasts XRP transfers in-app.
Spectra signs and broadcasts supported %: Spectra signs and broadcasts supported %@ transfers. This preview is the live nonce and fee estimate for the transaction you are about to send.
Spectra stores fee priority separately f: Spectra stores fee priority separately for each UTXO chain and applies it to live send previews for supported chains.
Spectra will prioritize non-custodial st: Spectra will prioritize non-custodial staking workflows where you keep control of your keys and can choose how your stake supports network decentralization.
Stake assets while preserving transparen: Stake assets while preserving transparent, decentralized consensus.
Staking helps secure proof-of-stake netw: Staking helps secure proof-of-stake networks by distributing validator power across many independent participants instead of relying on a centralized operator.
Stellar addresses start with G and are S: Stellar addresses start with G and are StrKey encoded.
TON addresses are usually user-friendly: TON addresses are usually user-friendly strings like UQ... or raw 0:<hex> addresses.
Tap the + button in the top right to add: Tap the + button in the top right to add your first wallet.

import_flow.create_chain_selection_subti: Each selected chain becomes its own wallet from the same seed phrase.
import_flow.import_chain_selection_subti: Seed imports can create one wallet per selected chain.
import_flow.seed_phrase_invalid_words_fo: These words are not valid BIP-39 English words: %@.
import_flow.wallet_name_hint: Leave this blank to let Spectra name it automatically.
import_flow.wallet_password_explanation: Add a password if you want Spectra to ask for it before revealing this wallet's seed phrase later. Leave it blank if you don't want that extra step.
import_flow.wallet_password_subtitle: Add an optional password if you want Spectra to ask for it before revealing this wallet's seed phrase later.
import_flow.wallet_password_success: This password will be required when showing the seed phrase for this wallet.
